title: Function
statement:
Let $A$ and $B$ be sets.
A \textbf{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation $f \subseteq A \times B$
such that:
- [label=(\roman*)]
- $\forall a \in A,\; \exists b \in B,\; (a,b) \in f$;
- if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$, then $b_1=b_2$.
We write $f:A\to B$ and $f(a)=b$ in place of $(a,b)\in f$.
